results.input: ChileMine Ltd (raw_material_supplier, geopolitical risk score 6.2/10) halts all lithium carbonate shipments. KoreanCell Co depends solely on ChileMine Ltd and has 5 days safety stock. What is the risk level?
A tier-3 raw material supplier with geopolitical risk score 2.1 delays delivery by 2 days. The downstream buyer has 30 days buffer and 3 qualified alternates. Risk level?
A component manufacturer in South Korea (geopolitical risk score 4.5) faces a 30-day production halt. Its sole customer has 7 days safety stock and no qualified alternate suppliers. Risk level?

A lithium carbonate supplier with 45000 tonnes annual output halts production indefinitely due to sanctions. It is the sole global supplier of this grade. Risk level for downstream battery manufacturers?
Export controls target a region supplying 5% of global copper. Three qualified alternate suppliers exist globally. Downstream manufacturers have 20-day buffer stock. Risk level?
A component manufacturer's ERP system goes offline for 48 hours due to a cybersecurity breach. Orders can be placed manually. Buffer stock covers 12 days. Risk level?
A sole-source tier-3 lithium supplier with high geopolitical risk declares force majeure for 90 days. Downstream tier-1 has 7 days stock. No qualified alternates exist. Risk level?
Port congestion adds a 5-day delay to a regular shipment. The manufacturer has 18 days buffer stock and two alternative ports. Risk level?
A raw material supplier with surge capacity multiplier 1.5x faces a partial disruption at 60% capacity. A downstream manufacturer with 10 days safety stock and one qualified alternate. Risk level?

A tier-1 automotive manufacturer (sole customer of a key components supplier) receives notice that its supplier will shut down in 72 hours. The manufacturer has 3 days safety stock and no alternates. Risk level?
A logistics provider raises freight rates by 12%. Four alternative carriers are available at similar rates. Lead times remain unchanged. Risk level?
A tier-2 supplier faces a 45-day labor strike. The tier-1 that depends on it has 8 days buffer. Two qualified alternates each supply 40% of volume. Risk level?
Currency devaluation increases procurement costs by 38% from a key overseas sole-source supplier. No domestic alternative exists. Risk level?
A quality audit failure requires re-inspection of 3% defective inventory. Buffer stock covers 25 days. The supplier can resume full production in 5 days. Risk level?
Natural disaster destroys a sole-source facility producing a critical assembly component with 24-month rebuild time. No alternative production sites exist globally. Risk level?
A semiconductor fab reduces output by 15% due to maintenance. Three alternative fabs can absorb additional orders. Buyer has 30 days inventory. Risk level?

Geopolitical tensions cause a country providing 65% of global neon gas to impose export restrictions. Semiconductor fabs need neon for lithography. Alternative sources cover only 20% of demand. Risk level?
A cybersecurity breach at a logistics provider disrupts real-time shipment tracking for 72 hours. Physical shipments are unaffected. Buffer stock covers 15 days. Risk level?
A tier-3 steel supplier with a risk score of 3.2/10 delays a standard shipment by 4 days. Two qualified alternates exist. Buyer has 21 days buffer. Risk level?

Sanctions are imposed on a country supplying 80% of global lithium hydroxide. No qualified alternate producers exist at the required purity grade. Downstream battery manufacturers have 10 days stock. Risk level?
